places.name: San Bernardino alle Ossa
places.description: An ossuary built in 1210 to collect bones from the Ospedale Maggiore of Milan.

places.name: Bar Luce
places.description: A Milanese café entirely designed by the American director Wes Anderson.

places.name: L.O.V.E.
places.description: This giant middle finger statue sends an unambiguous message to Italy's bankers.

places.name: BackDoor 43
places.description: One of the world’s smallest bars, just 13 square feet, is brimming with speakeasy flair.

places.name: Loggia dei Mercanti Whispering Gallery
places.description: The secret medieval communication system will whisk your words above the noise of the crowd.

places.name: Albergo Diurno Venezia
places.description: Long-abandoned art nouveau baths beneath Milan's Piazza Oberdan.

places.name: Milan's Duomo "Dinosaur"
places.description: Milan's duomo is covered in beautiful carving including a curious "dinosaur-like" beast.

places.name: Bar Basso
places.description: Sip a Negroni Sbagliato in the bar that invented it.

places.name: La Vigna di Leonardo (Leonardo’s Vineyard)
places.description: Just across from the "Last Supper" painting is the famous painter's vineyard, a gift received for creating the masterpiece.
